themes.hook: India’s $250 billion IT sector is facing its 'Nokia moment.' It’s not because of a lack of talent—it’s because the billable hour is dying.
key_data_points: Traditional 'time and material' billing is being replaced by outcome-based pricing due to AI efficiencies.
Top Indian IT firms are seeing sharp market cap declines as investors price in AI cannibalization.
The shift moves the industry away from headcount-linked growth for the first time in 30 years.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The real threat of AI isn't that it replaces coders; it's that it destroys the 'linear' relationship between revenue and headcount. If a task takes 10 hours instead of 100, billing by the hour becomes a race to the bottom for margins.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you think Indian IT firms can successfully pivot to IP-led models, or will they remain service 'body shops' with permanently lower valuations?

source_articles.title: IT firms rethink billing as artificial intelligence reshapes work
Top Indian IT firms face sharp market cap declines amid AI disruption

themes.hook: Somewhere in the Kremlin, Putin is rubbing his hands. India is now paying ~$99/barrel for Russian Urals, and our 'inflation hedge' is officially evaporating.
key_data_points: Russian crude costs have hit ~$99/barrel including shipping, narrowing the discount significantly.
West Asia tensions are adding 'war risk' premiums and surcharges to Indian cargo.
Daily City Gas Distribution (CGD) sales volumes are projected to drop by 8-10% due to Middle East disruptions.
themes.non_obvious_angle: India's macro stability over the last two years was subsidized by cheap Russian oil. As that discount vanishes and shipping costs spike, we are losing our fiscal shield just as global energy volatility is peaking.
themes.your_take_prompt: What is the second-order impact on the Rupee if our energy import bill loses its 'discounted' status while the Strait of Hormuz remains a chokepoint?

source_articles.title: India is paying ~$99 a barrel for Russian crude Urals
Middle East disruptions to impact daily CGD sales volume by 8-10%
From Aluminium To Alphonso Mangoes: West Asia Tensions Are Paralysing India’s Export Engines

themes.hook: 21 million small borrowers just dropped out of the formal credit system. At the same time, SIP accounts are hitting record highs. India's economy is de-linking.
key_data_points: 21 million micro-borrowers have lost access to formal credit, signaling rising rural financial stress.
Microfinance ticket sizes are increasing, suggesting a shift toward higher-risk, larger-debt profiles.
SIP accounts continue to rise despite high closures, showing a massive financialization of urban savings.
themes.non_obvious_angle: This isn't just a K-shaped recovery; it's a structural divorce. We are seeing a 'sophisticated' urban class booking profits in equity markets while the rural bottom-of-the-pyramid is being pushed back into the hands of informal moneylenders.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is the rising ticket size in microfinance a sign of 'growth' or a desperate attempt by lenders to maintain margins as the borrower base shrinks?

source_articles.title: At bottom of the pyramid, 21 million small borrowers have dropped out
Microfinance sector undergoing structural transformation in ticket size composition
India SIP Accounts Rise Despite High Closures in Feb 2026

themes.hook: The era of the 'Asset Light' Indian startup is dead. If you want to survive the next decade, you have to actually build things.
key_data_points: Atomberg is pivoting from selling fans to manufacturing its own motors and compressors.
Ola Electric is raising ₹2,000 Cr specifically for its battery manufacturing arm.
Bajaj Electricals just acquired the Morphy Richards brand to move from licensee to owner.
themes.non_obvious_angle: Software moats are thinning. In a world of high competition and AI-driven efficiency, the only way to defend margins is backward integration. Startups are realizing that owning the 'hardware' and the 'IP' is the only way to reach IPO-level profitability.
themes.your_take_prompt: Kashish, do you see this 'hard-asset' pivot as a sign of maturity or a capital-intensive trap for companies that used to be tech-first?

source_articles.title: Atomberg’s next spin as it prepares for IPO: From selling fans to supplying components
Ola Electric Plans ₹2,000 Cr Fundraise For Battery Arm
Bajaj Electricals Acquires Morphy Richards Brand

themes.hook: National security is non-negotiable—until it costs you $100 billion in manufacturing. India is quietly making peace with Chinese capital.
key_data_points: The 10% automatic route threshold for FDI provides a 'fast track' for firms with minor Chinese shareholding.
DPIIT is easing Press Note 3 restrictions for critical sectors like electronics and rare earths.
India is simultaneously restricting 'cheap' silver jewellery imports to protect local artisans.
themes.non_obvious_angle: The 'China Plus One' strategy was hitting a wall because you cannot build a global electronics hub without the Chinese component ecosystem. This isn't a policy reversal; it's a 'strategic surrender' to the reality of global supply chains.
themes.your_take_prompt: Is this 10% threshold a 'slippery slope' for national security, or is it the only way to ensure 'Make in India' actually scales?

source_articles.title: DPIIT notifies changes in FDI norms for China, other land border sharing countries
Electronics, rare earths among sectors to get faster FDI approvals
India restricts imports of silver jewellery with cheap diamonds
